summary: Iteration 253 — verified (1) transfer_fund / intra_account_transfer now fall through _direction_of to debit_or_credit (the prod Madapur Warehouse case), (2) new PATCH /api/accounting/transactions/{id}/direction-override with credit/debit/auto + 400/404/auth validation, (3) override is RESPECTED by /reclassify-direction and survives. 25 new tests pass + 66 prior regression tests (iter 248/249/250/251/252) still pass = 91/91 PASS.

unit_tests_direction_of: transfer_fund + debit_or_credit='credit' -> 'credit' (THE PROD MADAPUR CASE — was 'debit' before)
transfer_fund + debit_or_credit='debit' -> 'debit' (source side of same transfer)
direction_override_endpoint: PATCH {direction:'credit'} -> 200 {ok,direction:'credit',override:'credit'}; DB row has direction='credit' AND direction_override='credit'
PATCH {direction:'auto'} -> 200 {override:None}; DB row direction recomputed from raw, direction_override field UNSET ($exists false)
override_survives_resync: Seed raw={expense,debit_or_credit:debit,amount:500} direction=debit, override to 'credit', call /reclassify-direction, verify direction still 'credit' and direction_override still 'credit' (NOT flipped back to 'debit')
regression: POST /accounting/transactions/sync responds <8s (200 with job_id, or 400 fast)
All 8 iter-248 + 14 iter-249 + 12 iter-250 + 11 iter-251 + 21 iter-252 prior tests still PASS

critical_code_review_comments: _CREDIT_TYPES / _DEBIT_TYPES (lines 46-47 of routes/accounting_transactions.py) correctly OMIT 'transfer_fund' and 'intra_account_transfer'. Comment at lines 40-45 documents the rationale (inherently directional per account). Clean.
_direction_of docstring (lines 99-118) documents both the iter-251 mistake AND the new override workflow — excellent breadcrumb for future maintainers.

_run_sync (lines 188-216) now pre-fetches `direction_override` for existing rows and ONLY writes `direction` into update_set when override is NOT set (line 213). Override-protection is enforced at sync time. ✓
/reclassify-direction (lines 370-407) projects `direction_override` and, if set, uses it as the desired direction (lines 386-393) instead of running _direction_of. Override is honoured here too. ✓

PATCH /{item_id}/direction-override (lines 415-453) — Pydantic body validation, lowercase+strip normalisation, 400 on invalid value (line 433-434), 404 on missing row (lines 436-438), admin gate via _require_admin (line 430), tenant-scoped query, and the 'auto' branch correctly uses $unset to remove the field entirely (line 445). Atomic single update_one. Returns {ok, direction, override} consistently. Clean implementation.

MINOR (carried from prior iterations): /app/backend/routes/accounting_transactions.py is now 972 lines — well past the 700-line guideline. A future refactor splitting into sync / list / tagging / proofs / diagnostic / override sub-modules would help. Non-blocking.

MINOR: PATCH endpoint accepts request body without explicit role mention — _require_admin gates it. Frontend integration should expose the override action only to admin/CEO/CFO roles to avoid 403s for ordinary staff.

success_rate.backend: 100% (91/91 tests passing — 25 new iter-253 + 21 iter-252 + 11 iter-251 + 12 iter-250 + 14 iter-249 + 8 iter-248)

test_credentials: surya.yadavalli@nylaairwater.earth / test123 (CEO, tenant nyla-air-water). X-Tenant-ID header required.
seed_data_creation: Per-class autouse fixtures seed TEST-ITER253-* docs into accounting_transactions and clean them up. Used pymongo (sync) for direct DB seed/read to avoid motor event-loop binding issues. No persistent test data left in DB.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 253 removes 'transfer_fund' and 'intra_account_transfer' from _DEBIT_TYPES (they now fall through to debit_or_credit), and adds PATCH /api/accounting/transactions/{id}/direction-override (credit/debit/auto). Override is a row-level field (direction_override) honoured by BOTH the background sync (_run_sync skips overwriting `direction` when override is set) AND the /reclassify-direction maintenance endpoint. 'auto' uses $unset to remove the field. The diagnostic endpoint and all iter-248..252 regressions still pass. When writing new tests, prefer pymongo (sync) for direct DB seeding (the project's motor instance is single-event-loop bound and will hang when reused across pytest's loop boundary).
rca_of_the_issue: Prod reported a Zoho transfer (transaction_type='transfer_fund') from 'Director-loan Vamshi Krishna Bommena' INTO 'Madapur Warehouse' showing as money-OUT for Madapur. RCA: _DEBIT_TYPES included 'transfer_fund' (and 'intra_account_transfer'), but a Zoho transfer is inherently DIRECTIONAL per account — money-in for the destination, money-out for the source. The single transaction_type cannot encode this; Zoho's per-account debit_or_credit must. Fix: removed those two values from both allowlists so _direction_of falls through to debit_or_credit (statement convention: 'credit'=money in). Verified by unit test: {transaction_type:'transfer_fund', debit_or_credit:'credit'} -> 'credit' (Madapur's POV), and {…, debit_or_credit:'debit'} -> 'debit' (loan account's POV). Companion feature: PATCH /direction-override lets the CEO/admin permanently fix any one row that still mis-classifies (e.g. when Zoho's debit_or_credit is itself wrong). Override is persisted as direction_override on the row and respected by both /reclassify-direction (line 386-393) and _run_sync (line 213), so the user never has to fix the same row twice.
